title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds runtime-validated extraction contract from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of runtime-validated extraction contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Represent absent, empty, malformed, duplicate, conflicting, and admitted values as distinct outcomes.
Keep attributes, text, JSON-LD, configuration, and plugin output unknown until validators produce domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 7988 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Contract, keep Selector Contract connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
